g.text: epicd — Execution engine (class diagram; symmetric to authoring, invariant-heavy tail)
executionPipeline states = phases, each with actor (machine|human|none).
ready+in-progress by claim; needs-human IS a phase (actor=human).
scan predicate → pipelineDef[phase].actor=machine ∧ no live claim.
plan(task) : WorkSpec '' what to run; driver spawns the worker
flagStale() : void '' informational only; human decides
claims() : Map<taskId, Claim> '' SAME runtime-fact channel as authoring
scan → dispatch → worker → result flows back
as DATA → engine reads & branches. Adjudicator's DoD verdict is to execution
what the architect Reviewed verdict is to authoring — both appended data,
never a worker's own claim of success.
the completion tail (pink) is NOT a strategy — it is fixed
safety machinery (ENG-3 merge lock, ENG-8 no self-attested done). Variability
lives ONLY in WorkAction (role/kind). Do NOT make the tail pluggable.
TODAY: Interpreter + executionPipeline are pure & TEST-ONLY (unwired).
SafetyMonitor / Coordinator ALL ABSENT — soak runs on baime scan-loop.
